author: y4rr
content: Audit follow-up #5/5 llama-B (#698) APPROVE — opus 4.7 closed the remaining ferrotorch-llama findings (4 NumCast unwraps in hot path → cast<>?; 5 grammar panic sites resolved as Category C with substantive enriched-invariant expect messages naming upstream guards; GPU smoke test under #[cfg(all(test, feature='cuda'))] running on real RTX 3090 hardware with shape/finiteness/variance asserts; # Panics rustdoc on forward_from_ids/generate/generate_with_streamer/beam_search updated to honest 'Does not panic'). Discipline highlight: per-site reassessment on #3 actually happened — all 5 sites turned out to be Category C, but the analysis cited specific line numbers (~967 guard, ~755 apply_step transition, ~895 pop-on-empty, ~978 char accumulation) per site. Smoke test asserts have teeth: shape + per-element finiteness + non-degeneracy (variance > 0). Cross-dispatch protections intact: zero modifications to FerrotorchError taxonomy, zero modifications to StepError variants, llama-A's lints baseline + thiserror + non_exhaustive + manual Debug + categorical map_gpu_err all UNCHANGED. cargo test --features cuda --test gpu_smoke PASSED in 0.30s on real hardware (Step 0 probe confirmed CUDA 13.1 + driver 591.86). ferrotorch-llama audit FULLY CLOSED across 2 dispatches (A + B). Architect-mode running tally: 15 dispatches, 13 clean APPROVEs (item 8, item 5, mps, core-2a, core-2b, vision-A, vision-B, optim-A r2, optim-B, optim-C1, optim-C2, llama-A, llama-B), 2 truncations resolved via continuation (core-1, optim-A-1). CRATES DONE: 5/5 (mps + core + vision + optim + llama). User's master directive complete. Tracked follow-ups: #687 (real GPU has_inf_nan_f32 kernel), #692 (with_* builders for 17 optim configs), #694 (5 SAFETY-flagged optim API-shape concerns), #696 (Optimizer::state_dict trait → FerrotorchResult), #699 (FerrotorchError::Gpu source-chain variant).
